data.reasoningText:
**Analyzing current design flaws**

I'm focused on injecting some much-needed "wow factor" into the existing design. The core authentication logic, particularly `useLocalUser`, is solid and will stay. The sign-in button remains the pivotal call to action. I want to highlight the skills to be shown off, how to inject some real panache into the design.


**Defining Visual Direction**

I've sketched out the initial design framework, and I'm leaning heavily towards a split-screen layout. The left side, a dark and dynamic space, will showcase the service's value with an animated background. A large, bold headline and engaging visual elements will set the tone. The login form, taking the right side, will be a clean, glassmorphic panel.


**Planning animation implementation**

The core structure is set, so the focus shifts to animation. I'll test for `framer-motion` to handle those animations, otherwise Tailwind and CSS will suffice. The moving gradient background and hero text are the first items on the animation list. I can't wait to see how it looks!

**Analyzing Design Choices**

I've decided to prioritize a streamlined approach, focusing on `tailwindcss`, `tw-animate-css`, and standard CSS, as `framer-motion` isn't a dependency. This keeps things efficient. I also intend to use `lucide-react` for icons. I'll now redesign `app/routes/home.tsx`.


**Defining Implementation Strategy**

I'm now detailing the implementation plan for the redesign. The background will be either a gradient mesh or an SVG pattern. The hero section will feature the headline and subheadline, plus floating Lucide icons. I'll maintain the authentication card design, placing the "Sign in with EduAI" button front and center. I plan to use existing components like `card-editorial` and `btn-primary`, and highlight three features below the fold, each with a corresponding Lucide icon.


**Structuring the Homepage Redesign**

Now, I'm focusing on the structural details. For the home page, I'll use a split-screen layout on desktop, with hero content and visualizations on the left and the login form on the right. Mobile will stack these elements. I'll implement a floating effect using `animate-float` or custom CSS, if available. I'll stick with the planned features, utilizing the existing `card-editorial` and `btn-primary` components. Now, time to start coding the `app/routes/home.tsx` file!
